calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Oct-18-2016 Off Calendar

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-27-2016 Continued To Oct-25-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
MINI MINUTES: No appearance by Plaintiff. No sanctions. Chris Vaca, Esq. appearing for Defendant. The CRC 3.1385 Order to Show Cause is re-set to Oct-25-2016 at 10:30 AM in Department 610. JUDGE JOHN K. STEWART; Clerk RWoods. Proceedings not reported. (610)

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Dec-08-2015 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jul-26-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-07-2015 Set For Trial On Jan-04-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Jun-29-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Nicolas C. Vaca, of Law Office of Nicolas C. Vaca, Tel. 925.876.8709, for defendants;
No other appearances in the matter.

Ruling: The matter was not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT GUILLERMO BARRENCHEA'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT and DEFAULT JUDGMENT is granted. A trial court has broad discretion to vacate a default judgment. However, that discretion can be exercised only if defendant establishes a proper ground for relief, by the proper procedure, within time limits. (See Cruz v. Fagor America, Inc. (2006) 146 CA4th 488, 495.] Under Code Civ. Proc., Sec. 473, subd. (d), a court may set aside a default judgment which is valid on its face, but void, as a matter of law, due to improper service. (See Ellard v. Conway (2001) 94 Cal. App. 4th 540.] A trial court has no statutory power to set aside a judgment that is not void once six months have elapsed since the entry of a judgment. (See Cruz v. Fagor America, Inc. (2006) 146 CA4th 488, 495-96.) A judgment is said to be void on its face when the invalidity is apparent upon an inspection of the judgment roll. Where defendant does not answer the complaint, the judgment roll includes the proof of service. (Id. at 496.) Here, 17 months have elapsed since the entry of default judgment on January 23, 2014. However, Defendant has provided evidence that his address at the time of service was in San Jose, California, not Sunnyvale, California as reflected in the proof of service. Further, Defendant was served by substituted service and by mail, he was not personally served. Therefore, this court finds service to be invalid. Order signed in open court. Judge Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/JMQ)

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Jun-16-2015 Continued To Jun-29-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT GUILLERMO BARRENCHEA'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND is continued to June 29, 2015, at 9:30 a.m., in Dept. 302, on the Court's own motion. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)
